title: practical english usage - Part 95

verb form (e.g. works, goes). For information about noun-verb agreement in 1.  Singular noun-phrase subjects are generally used with a special present-tense agreement (e.g. horse's, microphones').

Besides singular and plural forms, nouns can also have possessive forms possessive and the second countable.

Aggression and attack express similar ideas, but the first is generally uncountable as a mass or as separate strands: English chooses to make wool uncountable.
